<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
    
    void serialise(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
        // Write packet ID
        stream.put(&packet_id, sizeof(packet_id));
        
        // Write username with fixed-length prefix
        const auto& username = this->username;
        int username_len = username.size();
        stream.put(&username_len, sizeof(username_len));
        stream.put(username.data(), username_len);
        
        // Write scores count and elements
        const auto& scores = this->scores;
        int scores_count = scores.size();
        stream.put(&scores_count, sizeof(scores_count));
        for (const auto& score : scores) {
            stream.put(&score, sizeof(score));
        }
        
        // Write null-terminated metadata
        stream << hexi::null_terminated(this->metadata);
    }
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_packet(const NetworkPacket& packet) {
    hexi::dynamic_buffer buffer;
    hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer);
    packet.serialise(stream);
    return { reinterpret_cast<const uint8_t*>(buffer.read_ptr()), buffer.size() };
}
```</answer>

But this code uses manual calculation of offsets, which is error-prone. However, given that the problem requires using the Hexi library, perhaps this is the way to go.

Now, putting all this into code.

For deserialize_packet:

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
};

NetworkPacket deserialize_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<read> reader(buffer);

    NetworkPacket packet;

    // Read packet_id
    uint32_t packet_id = 0;
    reader.get(&packet_id, sizeof(packet_id));
    packet.packet_id = hexi::endian::le_to_native(packet_id);

    // Read username with 4-byte prefix
    int username_len = 0;
    reader.get(&username_len, sizeof(username_len));
    username_len = hexi::endian::le_to_native(username_len);
    if(username_len < 0) {
        // Handle error
        packet.username = "";
    } else {
        packet.username.resize_and_overwrite(username_len, [&](char* strbuf, std::size_t size) {
            reader.get(strbuf, size);
            return size;
        });
    }

    // Read scores count
    uint32_t scores_count = 0;
    reader.get(&scores_count, sizeof(scores_count));
    scores_count = hexi::endian::le_to_native(scores_count);

    // Read scores into vector
    packet.scores.resize_and_overwrite(scores_count, [&](char* data, std::size_t size) {
        reader.get(data, size);
        return size;
    });

    // Read metadata as null terminated
    auto pos = reader.find_first_of(std::byte{0});
    if(pos == reader.npos) {
        packet.metadata = "";
    } else {
        packet.metadata.resize_and_overwrite(pos, [&](char* strbuf, std::size_t size) {
            reader.get(strbuf, pos);
            return size;
        });
        // Skip null terminator
        reader.skip(1);
    }

    // Skip padding
    const auto min_req_size = 4 + 4 + username_len + 4 + scores_count * 4 + (pos + 1); // pos is metadata length, +1 for null terminator
    if(reader.size() > min_req_size) {
        reader.skip(reader.size() - min_req_size);
    }

    return packet;
}

struct PacketInfo {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string_view username;
};

PacketInfo extract_packet_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<read> reader(buffer);
    PacketInfo info;

    // Read packet_id via span
    const auto packet_id_span = reader.span(0, 4);
    memcpy(&info.packet_id, packet_id_span.data(), 4);
    info.packet_id = hexi::endian::le_to_native(info.packet_id);

    // Read username with 4-byte prefix
    int username_len = 0;
    reader.get(&username_len, sizeof(username_len));
    username_len = hexi::endian::le_to_native(username_len);
    if(username_len < 0) {
        info.username = "";
    } else {
        const auto username_data = reader.data() + 8; // 4 (packet_id) +4 (prefix)
        info.username = { reinterpret_cast<const char*>(username_data), username_len };
    }

    // Read scores count and skip data
    uint32_t scores_count = 0;
    reader.get(&scores_count, sizeof(scores_count));
    scores_count = hexi::endian::le_to_native(scores_count);
    const auto scores_size = scores_count * sizeof(uint32_t);
    reader.skip(scores_size);

    return info;
}

But wait, the extract function's code for the username may not be correct. Because after reading the packet_id (4 bytes), the username prefix is at offset 4. So the username data starts at 4 +4 =8. So the code for username_data is correct.

Also, in the extract function, after reading the username_len, we create a string_view from the data at offset 8, with length username_len. But the buffer_adaptor's data() returns a pointer to the underlying buffer, which is the original buffer passed to the function. So this should be correct.

However, the string_view's lifetime is tied to the underlying buffer. Since the buffer is a span<const uint8_t>, and the string_view is created from it, this is safe as long as the buffer is not modified. Which it isn't, since it's a span to a const buffer.

For the metadata in deserialize_packet, after finding the null terminator, we read the data up to that point into the metadata string, then skip the null terminator. Then, the padding is calculated as the sum of all the parts plus the metadata length and null terminator, and any remaining bytes are skipped.

But the calculation of min_req_size may be incorrect. For example, the username_len is the length read from the prefix, which is already converted to native endianness. The pos is the position of the null terminator in the metadata, which is the length of the metadata. So min_req_size is 4 (packet_id) +4 (username prefix) + username_len (username) +4 (scores count) + scores_count*4 (scores) + pos (metadata) +1 (null terminator). The total required size is this sum. The actual buffer size may be larger, so the padding is buffer.size() - min_req_size. So if the buffer is larger than min_req_size, we skip the difference.

But this code assumes that the buffer is correctly formatted. Error handling is not included, as per the problem statement.

This should be the correct implementation.

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <vector>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct NetworkPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string username;
    std::vector<uint32_t> scores;
    std::string metadata;
};

NetworkPacket deserialize_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<read> reader(buffer);
    NetworkPacket packet;

    // Read packet_id
    uint32_t packet_id = 0;
    reader.get(&packet_id, sizeof(packet_id));
    packet.packet_id = hexi::endian::le_to_native(packet_id);

    // Read username with 4-byte prefix
    int username_len = 0;
    reader.get(&username_len, sizeof(username_len));
    username_len = hexi::endian::le_to_native(username_len);
    if (username_len > 0) {
        packet.username.resize_and_overwrite(username_len, [&](char* strbuf, std::size_t size) {
            reader.get(strbuf, size);
            return size;
        });
    }

    // Read scores count
    uint32_t scores_count = 0;
    reader.get(&scores_count, sizeof(scores_count));
    scores_count = hexi::endian::le_to_native(scores_count);

    // Read scores into vector
    packet.scores.resize_and_overwrite(scores_count, [&](char* data, std::size_t size) {
        reader.get(data, size);
        return size;
    });

    // Read metadata as null-terminated string
    auto pos = reader.find_first_of(std::byte{0});
    if (pos != reader.npos) {
        packet.metadata.resize_and_overwrite(pos, [&](char* strbuf, std::size_t size) {
            reader.get(strbuf, pos);
            return size;
        });
        reader.skip(1); // Skip null terminator
    }

    // Skip padding
    const auto min_req_size = 4 + 4 + username_len + 4 + scores_count * 4 + (pos + 1);
    if (reader.size() > min_req_size) {
        reader.skip(reader.size() - min_req_size);
    }

    return packet;
}

struct PacketInfo {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::string_view username;
};

PacketInfo extract_packet_info(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<read> reader(buffer);
    PacketInfo info;

    // Read packet_id via span
    const auto packet_id_span = reader.span(0, 4);
    memcpy(&info.packet_id, packet_id_span.data(), 4);
    info.packet_id = hexi::endian::le_to_native(info.packet_id);

    // Read username with 4-byte prefix
    int username_len = 0;
    reader.get(&username_len, sizeof(username_len));
    username_len = hexi::endian::le_to_native(username_len);
    if (username_len > 0) {
        const auto username_data = reader.data() + 8; // 4 (packet_id) + 4 (prefix)
        info.username = { reinterpret_cast<const char*>(username_data), username_len };
    } else {
        info.username = "";
    }

    // Read scores count and skip data
    uint32_t scores_count = 0;
    reader.get(&scores_count, sizeof(scores_count));
    scores_count = hexi::endian::le_to_native(scores_count);
    const auto scores_size = scores_count * sizeof(uint32_t);
    reader.skip(scores_size);

    return info;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>

struct UserData {
    uint64_t user_id;
    uint8_t age;
    std::string name;
    
    void serialise(auto& stream) {
        stream << user_id << age << name;
    }

    void deserialise(auto& stream) {
        stream >> user_id >> age >> name;
    }
};

UserData deserialize_user_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::exception_tag_t except_tag {};
    hexi::allocators/block_allocator allocator {};
    hexi::detail::storage storage(buffer.data(), buffer.size());
    hexi::stream_reader reader(storage, &except_tag, &allocator);

    UserData user;
    reader >> user.user_id;
    reader >> user.age;
    reader >> user.name; // Assumes the stream_reader handles prefixed string with a fixed-length prefix
    return user;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <hexi/exception.h>
#include <hexi/stream_adaptors.h>
#include <span>
#include <cstdint>

struct MessageHeader {
    uint16_t message_type;
    uint32_t body_length;
    uint32_t footer;
};

MessageHeader read_header_and_skip_body(const std::span<const std::byte> buffer) {
    MessageHeader header{};
    
    try {
        // Read message_type (2 bytes)
        uint16_t message_type = 0;
        hexi::read(&buffer[0], &message_type, sizeof(message_type));
        header.message_type = hexi::endian::le_to_native(message_type);

        // Read body_length (4 bytes)
        uint32_t body_length = 0;
        hexi::read(&buffer[sizeof(message_type)], &body_length, sizeof(body_length));
        header.body_length = hexi::endian::le_to_native(body_length);

        // Skip body_data
        if (buffer.size() < (sizeof(message_type) + sizeof(body_length) + body_length + sizeof(header.footer))) {
            HEXI_THROW(hexi::exception("Buffer underrun"));
        }
        hexi::skip(const_cast<std::span<std::byte>>(buffer).subspan(
            sizeof(message_type) + sizeof(body_length), body_length));

        // Read footer (4 bytes)
        uint32_t footer = 0;
        hexi::read(&buffer[sizeof(message_type) + sizeof(body_length) + body_length], 
                  &footer, sizeof(footer));
        header.footer = hexi::endian::le_to_native(footer);
    } catch (...) {
        // Clear in case an error occurs during partial read
        header.message_type = static_cast<uint16_t>(-1);
        header.body_length = static_cast<uint32_t>(-1);
        header.footer = static_cast<uint32_t>(-1);
        HEXI_THROW();
    }

    return header;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <cstdint>
#include <span>
#include <string_view>

struct PacketHeader {
    uint8_t version;
    uint8_t flags;
    uint16_t packet_id;
};

PacketHeader read_packet_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    const auto header_data = buffer.first<4>();
    return *reinterpret_cast<const PacketHeader*>(header_data.data());
}

std::string_view read_message_until_delimiter(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    const auto data = buffer.data();
    const auto len = buffer.size();
    for (size_t i = 0; i < len; ++i) {
        if (data[i] == 0) {
            return { reinterpret_cast<const char*>(data + 0), i };
        }
    }
    return { reinterpret_cast<const char*>(data), len };
}
```</answer>

    }
    result.data_type = buffer[0];
    if(buffer.size() < 5) { // 1 byte data_type + 4 bytes item_count

    // Read item_count from next 4 bytes
    hexi::buffer_adaptor< const std::span<const uint8_t> > adaptor(buffer.subspan(1));

    stream >> item_count;
    
    // Check if there's enough data for items
    const auto min_req_size = 1 + 4 + item_count * 4;
    if(buffer.size() < min_req_size) {

        stream = hexi::binary_stream(items_adaptor);

        hexi::buffer_adaptor< const std::span<const uint8_t> > items_adaptor(items_buffer);
        stream = hexi::binary_stream(items_adaptor);
        stream >> result.float_items;
    } else {
        // invalid data_type, handle error?
        return result;
    }
    
    return result;
}

DataResult preview_and_read_data(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    DataResult result;
    if (buffer.empty()) {
        return result;

    result.data_type = buffer[0];

        return result;
    }

    // Read item_count from the next 4 bytes
    const auto countBuffer = buffer.subspan(1, 4);
    hexi::buffer_adaptor<const std::span<const uint8_t>> adaptor(countBuffer);
    uint32_t item_count = 0;
    hexi::binary_stream stream(adaptor);
    stream >> item_count;

    const auto minRequiredSize = 5 + item_count * 4;
    if (buffer.size() < minRequiredSize) {
        return result;
    }

    // Read items based on data_type
    const auto itemsBuffer = buffer.subspan(5);
    if (result.data_type == 0) {
        result.int_items.resize(item_count);
        hexi::buffer_adaptor<const std::span<const uint8_t>> itemsAdaptor(itemsBuffer);
        stream = hexi::binary_stream(itemsAdaptor);
        stream >> result.int_items;
    } else if (result.data_type == 1) {
        result.float_items.resize(item_count);
        hexi::buffer_adaptor<const std::span<const uint8_t>> itemsAdaptor(itemsBuffer);
        stream = hexi::binary_stream(itemsAdaptor);
        stream >> result.float_items;
    }

    return result;
}

<think>Okay, I need to implement this function using the Hexi library. Let's see. The function is supposed to write data to a buffer after checking if there's enough space. The buffer has a header (a uint32_t) followed by a vector of uint32_t items. 

First, I remember that Hexi provides buffer_adaptors and binary_stream for serializing data. So the plan is to calculate how much space we need, check if the buffer has enough, then write the header and the items.

Wait, the steps are: check available space, get write pointer, write container data. So the function needs to first compute the required size. The header is 4 bytes. Each item in the vector is 4 bytes. So total size needed is 4 + (number of items * 4). 

But wait, the items are a vector of uint32_t. So if there are N items, that's N*4 bytes. So total required is 4 + N*4. 

Then, check if the buffer's size is at least that. If not, return false. Otherwise, proceed.

To write the data, use the binary_stream with the buffer_adaptor. But how exactly?

Let me think. The buffer is a std::vector<uint8_t>. We can create a hexi::buffer_adaptor for it. Then, using a hexi::binary_stream, write the header as a uint32_t, then write the items as a container.

Wait, but the binary_stream can write POD types directly. So for the header, we can write it as a single uint32_t. For the items vector, we can use the write_container function, which writes each element in the vector.

So the steps in code would be:

1. Calculate the required size: sizeof(header) + items.size() * sizeof(uint32_t)
2. Check if buffer.size() >= required size. If not, return false.
3. Get a pointer to the writeable part of the buffer. Since the buffer is a std::vector<uint8_t>, we can use buffer.data() as the underlying memory.
4. Use hexi::buffer_adaptor to wrap the buffer, then create a hexi::binary_stream with it.
5. Write the header to the stream.
6. Write the items vector using write_container.

    hexi::exception_tag allow_throw {};

    hexi::stream_write_adaptor adaptor(buffer);
    data.serialise(adaptor);

    // Copy to std::vector

    buffer.copy(result.data(), result.size());
    return result;
}

#include <hexi/dynamic_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024>
std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_from_buffer(const hexi::dynamic_buffer<block_size>& buffer, size_t bytes_to_peek) {
    const auto data = buffer.read_ptr();
    const auto read_size = buffer.size();
    
    // Ensure we don't read beyond the available data
    const size_t read_len = read_size < bytes_to_peek ? read_size : bytes_to_peek;
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> destination(read_len);
    memcpy(destination.data(), data, read_len);

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/tls_block_allocator.h>
#include <hexi/detail/intrusive_storage.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size = 1024, size_t allocator_count = 100>
using tls_allocator_type = hexi::tls_block_allocator<hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>, allocator_count>;

template<size_t block_size, size_t allocator_count>
void enter_thread_with_allocator(tls_allocator_type<block_size, allocator_count>& allocator) {
    allocator.initialize();
}

template<size_t block_size, size_t allocator_count>
void exit_thread_with_allocator(tls_allocator_type<block_size, allocator_count>& allocator) {
    allocator.deinitialize();
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<typename T>
T convert_to_big_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::native_to_big(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_from_big_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_to_little_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::native_to_little(value);
}

template<typename T>
T convert_from_little_endian(T value) {
    return hexi::endian::little_to_native(value);
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <cstdint>

template<hexi::endian::conversion conv, typename T>
constexpr T convert_with_enum(T value) {
    switch(conv) {
        case hexi::endian::big_to_native:
            return hexi::endian::from_big(value);
        case hexi::endian::native_to_big:
            return hexi::endian::to_big(value);
        case hexi::endian::little_to_native:
            return hexi::endian::from_little(value);
        case hexi::endian::native_to_little:
            return hexi::endian::to_little(value);
        default:
            std::unreachable();
    }
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/detail/intrusive_storage.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

template<size_t block_size>
std::vector<uint8_t> preview_data_without_consuming(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    const auto read_ptr = storage.read_ptr();
    const size_t read_size = storage.size();
    if (read_size < bytes_to_preview) {
        // Assuming the function should return as much as possible; adjust if needed.
        bytes_to_preview = read_size;
    }
    std::vector<uint8_t> dest(bytes_to_preview);
    memcpy(dest.data(), read_ptr, bytes_to_preview);
    return dest;
}

template<size_t block_size>
struct BufferStatus {
    size_t readable_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferStatus<> check_buffer_status(const hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<1024>& storage) {
    BufferStatus<> status;
    status.readable_size = storage.size();
    status.free_space = storage.free();
    return status;
}

template<size_t block_size>
void reset_buffer(hexi::detail::intrusive_storage<block_size>& storage) {
    storage.clear();
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/file_buffer.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>
#include <filesystem>

std::vector<uint8_t> read_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_read) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path.string().c_str(), hexi::buffer_read);
    std::vector<uint8_t> target(bytes_to_read);
    file.read(target.data(), bytes_to_read);
    return target;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> preview_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, size_t bytes_to_preview) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path.string().c_str(), hexi::buffer_read);
    std::vector<uint8_t> target(bytes_to_preview);
    file.copy(target.data(), bytes_to_preview);
    return target;
}

void write_file_data(const std::filesystem::path& file_path, const std::vector<uint8_t>& data) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path.string().c_str(), hexi::buffer_write);
    file.write(data.data(), data.size());
}

size_t get_file_size(const std::filesystem::path& file_path) {
    hexi::file_buffer file(file_path.string().c_str(), hexi::buffer_read);
    return file.size();
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/static_buffer.h>

template<size_t buffer_size = 1024>
struct BufferInfo {
    size_t capacity;
    size_t current_size;
    size_t free_space;
};

BufferInfo<1024> get_buffer_info(const hexi::static_buffer<std::byte, 1024>& buffer) {
    BufferInfo<1024> info;
    info.capacity = 1024;
    info.current_size = buffer.size();
    info.free_space = info.capacity - info.current_size;
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/stream_base.h>
#include <hexi/shared.h>

size_t get_stream_size(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    return stream.size();
}

bool is_stream_empty(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    return stream.size() == 0;
}

struct StreamInfo {
    hexi::stream_state state;
    size_t buffer_size;
    bool is_empty;
};

StreamInfo get_stream_state_info(const hexi::pmc::stream_base& stream) {
    StreamInfo info;
    info.state = stream.state();
    info.buffer_size = stream.size();
    info.is_empty = (info.buffer_size == 0);
    return info;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::size_t find_byte_in_buffer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    const auto data = buffer->read_ptr();
    for (std::size_t i = 0, j = buffer->size(); i < j; ++i) {
        if (data[i] == value) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return buffer_read::npos;
}

std::size_t find_delimiter_position(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte delimiter) {
    const auto data = buffer->read_ptr();
    for (std::size_t i = 0, j = buffer->size(); i < j; ++i) {
        if (data[i] == delimiter) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    return buffer_read::npos;
}

bool check_buffer_contains(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer, std::byte value) {
    const auto data = buffer->read_ptr();
    for (const std::byte& byte : data) {
        if (byte == value) {
            return true;
        }
    }
    return false;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

const void* get_read_pointer(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    return buffer.read_ptr();
}

struct BufferState {
    std::size_t size;
    const void* read_ptr;
    bool is_empty;
};

BufferState get_buffer_state(hexi::pmc::buffer_adaptor<std::vector<std::byte>>& buffer) {
    BufferState state;
    state.size = buffer.size();
    state.read_ptr = buffer.read_ptr();
    state.is_empty = (state.size == 0);
    return state;
}
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>

void write_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream, const std::string& str) {
    stream << hexi::prefixed_varint(str);
}

std::string read_string_prefixed_varint(hexi::binary_stream& stream) {
    std::string_view sv;
    stream >> hexi::prefixed_varint(sv);
    return std::string(sv);
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <cstring>

using namespace hexi::detail;

bool check_regions_overlap(const void* src, std::size_t src_len, const void* dst, std::size_t dst_len) {

}

<think>Okay, I need to implement these three functions using the Hexi library. Let's start by looking at each function's requirements.

First, the extract_underrun_info function takes a buffer_underrun exception. From what I remember, buffer_underrun exceptions occur when trying to read more data from a buffer than is available. The details should include read_size (how much was requested), total_read (total bytes read so far), buff_size (current buffer size), and the error message.

Looking at the Hexi documentation or examples, buffer_underrun probably has member functions to access these values. Maybe something like e.read_size(), e.total_read(), e.buff_size(), and e.what() for the message.

Similarly, extract_read_limit_info deals with stream_read_limit exceptions. These happen when reading from a stream exceeds the read limit. The required fields are read_size, total_read, read_limit, and message. So the stream_read_limit exception should have read_size(), total_read(), read_limit(), and what().

For get_exception_message, it's straightforward. Any Hexi exception has a what() member function that returns the error message as a string. So just return e.what().

Now, structuring the ExceptionDetails. For buffer_underrun, the limit_or_size would be buff_size. For stream_read_limit, it's read_limit. So in the first function, set limit_or_size to e.buff_size(), and in the second, e.read_limit().

Let me check the Hexi exception classes. Assuming buffer_underrun has read_size(), total_read(), buff_size(), and what(). Same for stream_read_limit with read_size(), total_read(), read_limit(), and what().

So the code for extract_underrun_info would be:

read_size = e.read_size();
total_read = e.total_read();
limit_or_size = e.buff_size();
message = e.what();

Similarly for extract_read_limit_info:

read_size = e.read_size();
total_read = e.total_read();
limit_or_size = e.read_limit();
message = e.what();

And get_exception_message is just returning e.what().

Putting this into the functions. Need to make sure the parameters are correct. The functions are given references to the respective exceptions, so accessing their member functions should work.

Testing edge cases: what if the exceptions don't have those member functions? But according to the problem statement, they do. So proceed under that assumption.

So the code should look like:

Implementing each function by accessing the respective members and assigning to the struct fields.

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstddef>

struct ExceptionDetails {
    std::size_t read_size;
    std::size_t total_read;
    std::size_t limit_or_size;
    std::string message;
};

ExceptionDetails extract_underrun_info(const hexi::buffer_underrun& e) {
    return {
        e.read_size(),
        e.total_read(),
        e.buff_size(),
        e.what()
    };
}

ExceptionDetails extract_read_limit_info(const hexi::stream_read_limit& e) {
    return {
        e.read_size(),
        e.total_read(),
        e.read_limit(),
        e.what()
    };
}

std::string get_exception_message(const hexi::exception& e) {
    return e.what();
}

#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/concepts.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <hexi/exception.h>
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/stream_adaptors.h>
#include <ranges>
#include <vector>
#include <string>

struct CompactPacket {
    uint32_t packet_id;
    std::vector<std::string> tags;
    std::string data;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_compact_packet(const CompactPacket& packet) {
    hexi::dynamic_buffer buffer;

    // Serialize packet ID as 4-byte little-endian
    const auto id = hexi::endian::native_to_little(packet.packet_id);
    buffer.write(&id, sizeof(id));

    // Serialize tags count with prefixed_varint
    hexi::prefixed_varint_encode<std::size_t>(buffer, 1, packet.tags.size());

    // Serialize each tag with prefixed_varint
    for (const auto& tag : packet.tags) {
        hexi::prefixed_varint_encode<std::size_t>(buffer, 1, tag.size());
        buffer.write(tag.data().bytes(), tag.size());
    }

    // Serialize data with prefixed_varint
    hexi::prefixed_varint_encode<std::size_t>(buffer, 1, packet.data.size());
    buffer.write(packet.data.data(), packet.data.size());

    return buffer.to_vector();
}

CompactPacket deserialize_compact_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    CompactPacket packet{};
    hexi::exception_tag allow_throw {};
    auto buffer_adaptor = hexi::buffer_sequence(buffer.data(), buffer.size(), &allow_throw);

    // Deserialize packet ID
    std::uint32_t id = 0;
    buffer_adaptor.get(&id, sizeof(id));
    packet.packet_id = hexi::endian::little_to_native(id);

    // Deserialize tags count
    std::size_t tags_count = 0;
    hexi::prefixed_varint_decode<std::size_t>(buffer_adaptor, 1, &tags_count, allow_throw);

    packet.tags.resize(tags_count);

    // Deserialize each tag
    for (size_t i = 0; i < tags_count; ++i) {
        std::size_t tag_len = 0;
        hexi::prefixed_varint_decode<std::size_t>(buffer_adaptor, 1, &tag_len, allow_throw);

        if (tag_len > buffer_adaptor.size()) {
            HEXI_THROW(hexi::exception("Buffer underrun during tag deserialization"));
        }

        packet.tags[i].resize_and_overwrite(tag_len, [&](char* strbuf, std::size_t size) {
            buffer_adaptor.get(strbuf, size);
            return size;
        });
    }

    // Deserialize data
    std::size_t data_len = 0;
    hexi::prefixed_varint_decode<std::size_t>(buffer_adaptor, 1, &data_len, allow_throw);

    if (data_len > buffer_adaptor.size()) {
        HEXI_THROW(hexi::exception("Buffer underrun during data deserialization"));
    }

    packet.data.resize_and_overwrite(data_len, [&](char* strbuf, std::size_t size) {
        buffer_adaptor.get(strbuf, size);
        return size;
    });

    return packet;
}

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <string>
#include <cstdint>

struct PaddedData {
    uint16_t header;
    std::string data;
    uint32_t footer;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_with_padding(const PaddedData& packet) {
    hexi::dynamic_buffer buffer;

    // Write header (2 bytes)
    buffer.write(&packet.header, sizeof(packet.header));

    // Write data with 4-byte prefix (big-endian)
    const auto data_len = packet.data.size();
    uint32_t len = htonl(data_len);
    buffer.write(&len, sizeof(len));
    buffer.write(packet.data.data(), data_len);

    // Write 16-byte padding filled with 0x00
    hexi::fill<16> padding(0x00);
    buffer.write(padding.data, sizeof(padding.data));

    // Write footer (4 bytes)
    buffer.write(&packet.footer, sizeof(packet.footer));

    return buffer.to_vector();
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/allocators/block_allocator.h>
#include <vector>
#include <memory>

struct PoolObject {
    int id;
    double value;
    
    // Default constructor required by block_allocator
    PoolObject() : id(0), value(0.0) {}
    
    PoolObject(int i, double v) : id(i), value(v) {}
};

std::vector<PoolObject*> create_object_pool(hexi::block_allocator<PoolObject, 10>& allocator) {
    std::vector<PoolObject*> pool;
    
    PoolObject* obj1 = allocator.allocate();
    obj1->id = 1;
    obj1->value = 1.1;
    pool.push_back(obj1);
    
    PoolObject* obj2 = allocator.allocate();
    obj2->id = 2;
    obj2->value = 2.2;
    pool.push_back(obj2);
    
    PoolObject* obj3 = allocator.allocate();
    obj3->id = 3;
    obj3->value = 3.3;
    pool.push_back(obj3);
    
    return pool;
}
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_base.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstddef>

class CustomBuffer : public hexi::pmc::buffer_base {
    std::vector<std::byte> data_;
    
public:
    virtual std::size_t size() const {
        return data_.size();
    }

    virtual bool empty() const {
        return data_.empty();
    }
    
    // Helper method to add data (for testing)
    void add_data(const void* source, std::size_t length) {
        const std::byte* bytes = static_cast<const std::byte*>(source);
        data_.insert(data_.end(), bytes, bytes + length);
    }
    
    // Helper method to clear data (for testing)
    void clear() {
        data_.clear();
    }
};
```</answer>

#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_read_adaptor.h>
#include <hexi/pmc/buffer_write_adaptor.h>
#include <vector>
#include <cstdint>

std::vector<uint8_t> write_and_read_data(const std::vector<uint8_t>& input_data) {
    std::vector<uint8_t> buffer;
    hexi::pmc::buffer_write_adaptor write_adaptor(buffer);
    
    // Ensure buffer has enough free space
    if (write_adaptor.free() < input_data.size()) {
        buffer.resize(input_data.size());
    }
    
    // Write data to buffer
    write_adaptor.write(input_data.data(), input_data.size());
    
    // Read data back
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_adaptor(buffer);
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(input_data.size());
    read_adaptor.read(result.data(), result.size());
    
    return result;
}

std::vector<uint8_t> peek_data_without_advancing(const std::vector<uint8_t>& buffer_data, std::size_t peek_size) {
    hexi::pmc::buffer_read_adaptor read_adaptor(buffer_data);
    
    // Check if enough data is available
    if (read_adaptor.size() < peek_size) {
        return {};
    }
    
    std::vector<uint8_t> result(peek_size);
    read_adaptor.copy(result.data(), peek_size);  // Peek without advancing
    
    // Consume the data by advancing the read cursor
    read_adaptor.read(nullptr, peek_size);
    
    return result;
}
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#include <hexi/hexi.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <hexi/exception.h>
#include <hexi/endian.h>
#include <hexi/shared.h>
#include <hexi/stream_adaptors.h>
#include <vector>
#include <span>
#include <utility>
#include <cassert>
#include <cstddef>
#include <cstdint>

struct ProtocolPacket {
    uint32_t message_id;
    std::vector<uint16_t> data_values;
    uint32_t checksum;
};

std::vector<uint8_t> serialize_protocol_packet(const ProtocolPacket& packet) {
    hexi::exception_tag allow_throw {};
    hexi::allocators/block_allocator<>::init();
    
    try {
        hexi::binary_stream stream(hexi::stream_state::ok);
        
        // Write message_id in big-endian
        const auto swapped_id = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.message_id);
        stream.put(&swapped_id, sizeof(swapped_id));
        
        // Prepare and write data_values with endianness conversion
        std::vector<uint16_t> swapped_data;
        swapped_data.reserve(packet.data_values.size());
        for (const auto& value : packet.data_values) {
            swapped_data.push_back(hexi::endian::native_to_big(value));
        }
        stream.write_container(swapped_data);
        
        // Write checksum in big-endian
        const auto swapped_checksum = hexi::endian::native_to_big(packet.checksum);
        stream.put(&swapped_checksum, sizeof(swapped_checksum));
        
        return stream.str();
    } catch(...) {
        hexi::allocators/block_allocator<>::cleanup();
        throw;
    }
}

ProtocolPacket deserialize_protocol_packet(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    hexi::exception_tag allow_throw {};
    hexi::allocators/block_allocator<>::init();
    
    try {
        hexi::binary_stream stream(buffer, hexi::stream_state::ok);
        ProtocolPacket packet = {};
        
        // Read and convert message_id
        uint32_t swapped_id = 0;
        stream.get(&swapped_id, sizeof(swapped_id));
        packet.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(swapped_id);
        
        // Read data_values count
        uint32_t count = 0;
        stream.get(&count, sizeof(count));
        packet.data_values.resize_and_overwrite(count, [&](char* data, std::size_t size) {
            assert(size % sizeof(uint16_t) == 0);
            return size;
        });
        
        // Read and convert data_values
        stream.read_container(packet.data_values);
        for (auto& value : packet.data_values) {
            value = hexi::endian::big_to_native(value);
        }
        
        // Read and convert checksum
        uint32_t swapped_checksum = 0;
        stream.get(&swapped_checksum, sizeof(swapped_checksum));
        packet.checksum = hexi::endian::big_to_native(swapped_checksum);
        
        return packet;
    } catch(...) {
        hexi::allocators/block_allocator<>::cleanup();
        throw;
    }
}

struct ProtocolHeader {
    uint32_t message_id;
};

ProtocolHeader extract_protocol_header(const std::span<const uint8_t> buffer) {
    assert(buffer.size() >= sizeof(uint32_t));
    ProtocolHeader header{};
    
    // Zero-copy read of message_id
    std::memcpy(&header.message_id, buffer.data(), sizeof(uint32_t));
    header.message_id = hexi::endian::big_to_native(header.message_id);
    
    // Skip remaining header bytes (if any)
    // Assuming protocol header is exactly 4 bytes (message_id)
    // No need to skip additional bytes in this case
    
    return header;
}
```</answer>
